merger_name: Ochre Health - New Lambton Family Practice, NSW

targets.name: NEW LAMBTON FAMILY PRACTICE PTY LTD
Business and assets comprising New Lambton Family Practice
The trustee for Lual Services Trust

merger_description:
Ochre Health Pty Limited (**Ochre Health**) proposes to acquire the business and all of the assets of New Lambton Family Practice (**NLFP**) through a business and assets acquisition agreement.

The acquirer, Ochre Health, supports independent doctors to provide general practice medical services in regional, rural and urban communities around Australia. Ochre Health currently operates 69 medical centres and urgent care clinics located in the ACT, NSW, Queensland, Tasmania and Victoria. Ochre Health group’s medical recruitment business, Ochre Recruitment Pty Limited, also works with hospitals and medical practices across Australia and New Zealand to source and place locum and permanent doctors across a range of specialities.

Ochre Health is majority owned and controlled by Genesis Capital, an Australian-based private equity firm that invests in businesses operating in the healthcare sector.

The target, NLFP is a general practice medical service operating from its premises in New Lambton, New South Wales. NLFP is currently owned and operated by New Lambton Family Practice Pty Ltd and the trustee for Lual Services Trust.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Ochre Health Pty Ltd (Ochre Health) applied for a notification waiver
in respect of its proposed acquisition of all of the assets of New
Lambton Family Practice (NLFP) through a business and assets
acquisition agreement, as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Ochre Health, supports independent doctors to provide
general practice medical services in regional, rural and urban
communities around Australia. Ochre Health currently operates 69
medical centres and urgent care clinics located in the ACT, NSW,
Queensland, Tasmania and Victoria. Ochre Health group’s medical
recruitment business, Ochre Recruitment Pty Limited, also works
with hospitals and medical practices across Australia and New
Zealand to source and place locum and permanent doctors across a
range of specialities.
Ochre Health is majority owned and controlled by Genesis Capital
(Genesis) an Australian-based private equity firm that invests in
businesses operating in the healthcare sector. Genesis’s
investments include Holdsworth House Medical Group, which
operates two general medical practices in Brisbane and Sydney, with
a co-located medical research centre, specialist medical clinic and
dentistry clinic at its Sydney premises.
The target, NLFP is a general practice medical service operating
from its premises in New Lambton, a suburb of Newcastle in New
South Wales. NLFP is currently owned and operated by New
Lambton Family Practice Pty Ltd and the trustee for Lual Services
Trust.
Ochre Health, through Ochre Medical Centre Kotara, and NLFP
supply general practice medical services in Newcastle, NSW.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver and had regard
to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
• a. the aggregation arising from the Acquisition appears low
b. there are alternative suppliers of general practice medical
services in the New Lambton area, and in the broader
Newcastle area in NSW.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
